text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr A. Patel  
Riverside Medical Practice  
48 Mill Street, London SE1 3AB  

Dear Dr A. Patel,  

Mel Turner, DOB: 22-07-1984, Hospital No: NHS3849201 NHS No. UCLH-00291837  
Flat 3, 12 Ferry Lane, London, SE16 4QX 

Summary/ Diagnoses: History of seizures with cognitive complaints post sleep deprivation episodes; mood fluctuation under review; vitamin D insufficiency; ex-smoker; moderate caffeine intake. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.  
Clobazam 10 mg nocte as rescue for clusters (patient-directed protocol).  
Cholecalciferol 1000 IU daily.  
Omeprazole 20 mg daily.  

I reviewed Mel in clinic today. He attended alone, having brought his mobile phone with the seizure diary app that he has used consistently for the last 12 months. He reports improved sleep hygiene over the past quarter and has reduced caffeinated drinks to one in the morning. He walks daily and has returned to part-time work in a warehouse setting; no reported injuries. He notes occasional word-finding difficulty after poor sleep and mild irritability in the evenings, both settling with rest. No new neurological focal symptoms reported..

Seizures: We examined the diary app together, which shows clear date-stamped entries with brief free-text descriptions and automated monthly summaries. Over the past 9 months, events occurred at intervals ranging 20 to 30 days, typically in the early morning, lasting under 2 minutes by his estimate, with post-event fatigue for several hours. He has not required emergency services, and there have been no witnessed injuries. No clear precipitant identified beyond occasional late nights; alcohol intake is minimal (1–2 units/week). He has not used clobazam in the last 4 months. 

Examination: Alert and oriented. Speech fluent. Cranial nerves intact. Motor power 5/5 throughout, tone normal, no pronator drift. Reflexes symmetrical, plantar responses flexor. Sensation intact to light touch and pinprick. Gait steady, normal base, Romberg negative. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations unremarkable. 

Investigations: Last routine bloods (FBC, U&Es, LFTs, vitamin D) from July 2025 were within normal limits apart from borderline low vitamin D (now on supplementation). MRI brain (2023) unchanged from prior with no acute findings. No recent EEG performed. 

Plan:  
- Continue levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily without change.  
- Maintain seizure diary app logging; we will export a PDF summary before the next review.  
- Reinforce sleep regularity and hydration; reduce late-night screen exposure where possible.  
- No driving per DVLA guidance until criteria are fulfilled; patient confirms not currently driving.  
- Bloods (FBC, U&Es, LFTs, vitamin D) to be repeated via GP in 6 months.  
- Provide work letter confirming fitness for current duties with avoidance of lone work at heights.  
- Review in clinic in 6 months, sooner if event pattern changes (e.g., clusters, prolonged events, injuries) or adverse effects from medication arise.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: events occurred at intervals ranging 20 to 30 days

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly discusses seizure frequency in the Seizures section. It states: "Over the past 9 months, events occurred at intervals ranging 20 to 30 days." This indicates a recurring pattern with an interval range between seizures. Convert this to the allowed format as a frequency per month: since intervals are given in days, we keep it in days. The appropriate normalization is a range expressed as "1 per 20 to 30 day". There is no indication of complete seizure freedom for 6 months; therefore, not seizure free. No need to sum multiple types or adjust for clusters since no clusters reported in last 4 months and the frequency statement covers overall events.
seizure_frequency_number: events occurred at intervals ranging 20 to 30 days
